name: Frankie Knuckles
description: '''Francis Warren Nicholls Jr.''' (January 18, 1955 – March 31, 2014), known professionally as '''Frankie Knuckles''', was an American DJ, record producer, and remixer. He played an important role in developing and popularizing [[house music]], which originated in Chicago during the early 1980s and spread worldwide. In 1997, Knuckles won the [[Grammy Award for Remixer of the Year, Non-Classical]]. Knuckles was often called "the godfather of house music".
